title: leptocarpus ejuncidus
side_effects: Leptocarpus ejuncidus can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Ingesting the plant's toxic compounds can lead to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Prolonged exposure to its oils can cause skin irritation, redness, and inflammation. If not handled carefully, the plant's sap can cause eye irritation and temporary vision loss.
